meaning: adj.婴儿的,幼稚的,适于婴儿的初期的
examples: Very often, we do not completely outgrow that infantile notion that our wishes cause things to happen.
These three explanations of infantile amnesia are not mutually exclusive; indeed, they support each other.
Most people are not able to recount memories for experiences prior to the age of three years, a phenomenon called infantile amnesia.
Infantile spasms, a rare form of epilepsy, strike 2, 500 babies in the U.S. every year.
The dramatic possibilities of infantile bullies goading each other to violence are sadly limited.
